task: Create a Python function that validates if an AWS S3 bucket name follows the correct naming conventions: 3-63 characters long, contains only lowercase letters, numbers, dots, and hyphens, doesn't start or end with dots or hyphens, and doesn't contain consecutive dots.
solution_criteria: Function should return boolean, handle edge cases (empty strings, None), correctly validate all naming rules including no consecutive dots, and properly check start/end character restrictions

task: Generate a JSON policy document that grants read-only access to a specific S3 bucket named 'my-data-bucket' for objects with the prefix 'reports/' to any authenticated user.
solution_criteria: Must be valid IAM policy JSON with correct Version, Statement structure, appropriate Effect (Allow), correct Action for read-only S3 access (s3:GetObject), and properly formatted Resource ARN with bucket name and prefix

task: Write a regular expression that matches valid AWS IAM role ARNs. The ARN should start with 'arn:aws:iam::', followed by an account ID (12 digits), then ':role/', and end with a role name that can contain alphanumeric characters, plus (+), equal (=), comma (,), period (.), at (@), and hyphen (-) symbols.
solution_criteria: Regex should match the exact ARN format, validate 12-digit account ID, include all specified allowed characters for role names, and not match invalid ARN formats or incorrect account ID lengths
